questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A container holds $10^{26}$ molecules $/ \mathrm{m}^3$, each of mass $3 \times 10^{-27} \mathrm{~kg}$. Assume that $1 / 6$ of the molecules move with velocity $2000 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ directly towards one wall of the container while the remaining $5 / 6$ of the molecules move either away from the wall or in perpendicular direction, and all collisions of the molecules with the wall are elastic
options: Number of molecules hitting $1 \mathrm{~m}^2$ of the wall every second is $3.33 \times 10^{28}$.
Number of molecules hitting $1 \mathrm{~m}^2$ of the wall every second is $2 \times 10^{29}$.
Pressure exerted on the wall by molecules is $24 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~Pa}$.
Pressure exerted on the wall by molecules is $4 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~Pa}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The molar heat capacity for an ideal gas
options: is zero for an adiabatic process
is infinite for an isothermal process
depends only on the nature of the gas for a process in which either volume or pressure is constant
is equal to the product of the molecular weight and specific heat capacity for any process
questions.solution:
The molar heat capacity has the general definition
$C=\frac{1}{n} \cdot \frac{\Delta Q}{\Delta T}$
where $\mathrm{n}=$ number of moles, $\Delta \mathrm{Q}=$ heat absorbed by the gas and $\Delta \mathrm{T}=$ rise in temperature of gas.
It is possible to obtain almost any set of values for $\Delta \mathrm{Q}$ and $\Delta \mathrm{T}$ by proper selection of a process.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: From the following statements, conceming ideal gases at any given temperature T, select the correct one(s).
options: The coefficient of volume expansion at constant pressure is same for all ideal gases.
The average translational kinetic energy per molecule of oxygen is 3kT .
In a gaseous mixture, the average translational kinetic energy of the molecules of each component is same.
The mean free path of molecules increases with decrease in pressure.
questions.solution: See the assumptions of kinetic theory of gases.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: At ordinary temperatures, the molecules of an ideal gas have only translational and rotational kinctic energies. At higher temperatures, they may also have vibrational energy. As a result, at higher temperatures
questions.solution: $C_{\mathrm{v}}=\frac{\mathrm{f}}{2} R$, where $f$ is the degree of freedom
